ip_intent.one_line_desc: Manages device life cycle states and transitions, and controls key manager, NVM (flash), OTP, and debug access
ip_intent.one_paragraph_desc:
Life Cycle Controller is responsible for handling and guarding transitions between different device life cycle states, concurrently decoding the current life cycle state and redundantly broadcasting encoded life cycle qualification signals across the system, e.g., to control the behavior of the Key Manager, the non-volatile memory controller (e.g. Flash Controller), the OTP Controller, and the debug infrastructure.
In addition, it features an escalation receiver for the alert system to invalidate the life cycle state as part of an escalation sequence.

param_list.name: SecVolatileRawUnlockEn
param_list.desc:
Disable (0) or enable (1) volatile RAW UNLOCK capability.
If enabled, it is possible to perform a volatile RAW -> TEST_UNLOCKED0 transition
without programming the OTP. This is a useful fallback mode in case the OTP is
not working correctly.

IMPORTANT NOTE: This should only be used in test chips. The parameter must be set
to 0 in production tapeouts since this weakens the security posture of the RAW
UNLOCK mechanism.

param_list.name: UseDmiInterface
param_list.desc: When 1, a TLUL-based DMI interface is used. When 0, a JTAG TAP is used.

param_list.name: RndCnstLcKeymgrDivInvalid
param_list.desc: Diversification value used for all invalid life cycle states.

param_list.name: RndCnstLcKeymgrDivTestUnlocked
param_list.desc: Diversification value used for the TEST_UNLOCKED* life cycle states.

param_list.name: RndCnstLcKeymgrDivDev
param_list.desc: Diversification value used for the DEV life cycle state.

param_list.name: RndCnstLcKeymgrDivProduction
param_list.desc: Diversification value used for the PROD/PROD_END life cycle states.

param_list.name: RndCnstLcKeymgrDivRma
param_list.desc: Diversification value used for the RMA life cycle state.

param_list.name: RndCnstInvalidTokens
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits used for invalid tokens in the token mux

param_list.name: SiliconCreatorIdWidth
param_list.desc: Width of SiliconCreatorId revision field.

param_list.name: ProductIdWidth
param_list.desc: Width of ProductId revision field.

param_list.name: RevisionIdWidth
param_list.desc: Width of RevisionId revision field.

param_list.name: SiliconCreatorId
param_list.desc: Chip generation number.

param_list.name: ProductId
param_list.desc: Chip revision number.

param_list.name: RevisionId
param_list.desc: Chip revision number.

param_list.name: IdcodeValue
param_list.desc: JTAG ID code.

param_list.name: NumTokenWords
param_list.desc: Number of 32bit words in a token.

param_list.name: CsrLcStateWidth
param_list.desc: Number of life cycle state enum bits.

param_list.name: CsrLcCountWidth
param_list.desc: Number of life cycle transition counter bits.

param_list.name: CsrLcIdStateWidth
param_list.desc: Number of life cycle id state enum bits.

param_list.name: CsrOtpTestCtrlWidth
param_list.desc: Number of vendor/test-specific OTP control bits.

param_list.name: CsrOtpTestStatusWidth
param_list.desc: Number of vendor/test-specific OTP status bits.

param_list.name: NumDeviceIdWords
param_list.desc: Number of 32bit words in the Device ID.

param_list.name: NumManufStateWords
param_list.desc: Number of 32bit words in the manufacturing state.

param_list.name: NumRmaAckSigs
param_list.desc: Number of NVM RMA wiping response signals

param_list.name: EscNumSeverities
param_list.desc: Number of escalation severities

param_list.name: EscPingCountWidth
param_list.desc: Width of ping count for the escalation receiver

alerts.name: fatal_prog_error
alerts.desc: This alert triggers if an error occurred during an OTP programming operation.

alerts.name: fatal_state_error
alerts.desc: This alert triggers if an error in the life cycle state or life cycle controller FSM is detected.

alerts.name: fatal_bus_integ_error
alerts.desc: This fatal alert is triggered when a fatal TL-UL bus integrity fault is detected.
